status_detail: baidu real response body capture FAILED via multiple DevTools approaches. Chrome DevTools Network tab shows 'Nothing to preview / no response data available' for ALL conversation requests (normal and blocked), confirming this is a fundamental Chrome limitation with baidu's streaming. Console-based JS override (fetch + XMLHttpRequest + EventSource) also captured ZERO responses — suggesting baidu uses a less common streaming API (perhaps WebWorker/ServiceWorker, or custom transport), OR my Type input went to Console instead of chat input field during follow-up queries.

attempts.attempt_1_devtools_response_tab: Shows 'Nothing to preview / This request has no response data available' — same Chrome limitation confirmed from #508
attempts.attempt_3_full_override_xhr_es: FULL_OVERRIDE2_OK installed for XMLHttpRequest + EventSource + fetch, still 0 captures

hypothesis_about_baidu_transport: H1: Uses WebWorker or ServiceWorker for /aichat/api/conversation (explains DevTools opacity)
H2: Uses custom Deferred chunk processor that consumes bytes before DevTools can buffer
H3: Transport is fetch() but response.body is piped through ReadableStream that processes chunks inline (body text() never called, so DevTools doesn't retain it)
H4: My Type inputs went to Console prompt not chat input — no actual new query was made (but 理解问题 still displayed — could be from previous query)
recommended_dev_actions_FINAL: ⭐ PRIORITY 1: MITM capture at Etap server side. Add log that dumps raw response body bytes on /aichat/api/conversation for 5-10 unblocked queries. Write bytes to /var/log/ai_prompt/baidu_real_XX.bin. Then we can compare byte-for-byte with APF envelope.
Alternative: use chrome with --enable-logging --v=1 and inspect net logs for the conversation request (very verbose, but may capture body)
Alternative: inspect Sources > vendors-jSy077-j.js for 'fetch' / 'XMLHttpRequest' / 'Worker' references in processSSEContent's caller chain to identify exact transport

ULTIMATE FALLBACK: if envelope can't be made to match, use a completely different strategy — e.g., C++ generator produces a FULL HTML page (not SSE) with error message and appropriate Content-Type: text/html. HF-style SSE won't work for baidu; baidu has its own proprietary ChunkStreaming that we cannot reliably replicate without byte reference.
DOCUMENTED NEXT STEP: baidu v6-keepalive was last iteration. Next envelope variant should pause until server-side real response is captured.
notes_for_dev: We spent significant effort on baidu (6 envelope iterations + this capture attempt). The 5-strike rule already documented in #514. Chrome DevTools CANNOT capture baidu's response body — this is confirmed across multiple sessions and multiple test types. Server-side capture via Etap is the ONLY reliable path forward. Without real response reference bytes, further envelope guessing is unproductive. Recommend pausing baidu and moving to other services (gamma, qianwen) until Etap logging is ready.
retry_context: Final diagnostic attempt for baidu this session. All client-side capture methods exhausted. Definitive conclusion: need server-side response logging.
